items.title: Advocates say 'three big gorillas' have stopped real national gambling reform

children.content: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

gallery.altText: A Manly Sea Eagles jersey with rainbow bands above and below the sponsor name and on the cuffs and collar

items.title: Cattle industry loses court appeal over live export ban compensation

children.content: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

image.altText: the sun sets over cattle cattle yard with dust rising

items.title: Police to hold children up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes

children.content: Northern Territory Police will be able to hold children in watch houses for up to 48 hours and question them without an adult present under planned changes to the Youth Justice Act.

image.altText: Hands grasping a cyclone wire fence.

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

articles.title: Australia records first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade

children.content: The Northern Territory has recorded the country's first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade, as experts urge people to get vaccinated.

image.altText: The hallway inside a hospital's emergency department.

gallery.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

thumbnail.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: Man with swastika tattooed on leg arrested in Alice Springs

children.content: A 25-year-old man has been arrested and charged in Alice Springs for displaying a tattoo of a swastika on his leg.

image.altText: A patch of the NT police force emblem on an unidentified officer's uniform

items.title: National commissioner says review into Kumanjayi Little Baby won't 'cut it'

children.content: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

image.altText: Portrait of Yoorrook Commissioner Sue-Anne Hunter

items.title: Northern Territory musicians escape to international festival in the UK

children.content: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.

items.title: Outback cattle station granted permit to cull 20,000 native birds

children.content: A permit to cull 20,000 native birds has been granted to a cattle station in the Northern Territory in response to an "overpopulation" of destructive and "exceptionally noisy" galahs and little corellas.

image.altText: A flying pink and grey galah in soft light.

items.title: Runner takes on mountainous 231km to honour son's memory

children.content: Running hundreds of kilometres up and down the Larapinta Trail is a gruelling challenge most people will never attempt. But for Ben Mack, completing it would be a dream come true.

image.altText: A man wearing a legionnaires cap leans against a gum tree trunk.

items.title: What we know about the NT government's plan to change child protection laws

children.content: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

articles.title: NT government accused of 'politicising tragedy' with child protection laws

children.content: Peak Aboriginal bodies have accused the Northern Territory government of using Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged murder as political cover to push ahead with sweeping changes to child protection laws.

image.altText: A bald man with a beard and glasses and wearing a dark blue shirt looks to the left

articles.title: NT government announces details of review into Kumanjayi Little Baby's situation

children.content: The NT government has revealed the scope of a review into the territory's child protection system, and announced that it will be led by retired NSW police commissioner Karen Webb and long-standing territory public servant Greg Shanahan.

items.title: Five die following cave diving incident

children.content: The group was exploring a series of underwater caves at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll in the Maldives.

image.altText: Side-by-side images of a mother (left) and her daughter.

items.title: New Hollywood film excites as Philippine culture hits the big screen

children.content: As animated film Forgotten Island gets ready to hit the big screen later this year, could it signal an end to Filipino typecasting in Hollywood?

items.title: Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something bigger

children.content: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

image.altText: A graphic showing an ocean cutaway with satellites, buouys, drones and sensors trying to detect a submarine.

items.title: 'Grossly incompetent': Action taken against rogue MP after online outburst

children.content: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.

image.altText: Mark Latham in a black coat and orange time walking

items.title: Trump and Xi's tea in Beijing rewrites the century

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

articles.title: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing

children.content: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

image.altText: Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump walk in blue suits in front of five American and Chinese flags.

items.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools

children.content: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

image.altText: Jane Caro

items.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group

children.content: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Judge declares another mistrial in Harvey Weinstein rape case

children.content: Harvey Weinstein's third trial in New York on a charge that he raped aspiring actress Jessica Mann has ended in a mistrial, after a jury failed to reach a unanimous verdict.

items.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time

children.content: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

items.title: Zelenskyy vows retribution against Russia for missile strike that killed 24

items.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire despite new strikes

children.content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire and hold new talks on a political settlement, even as Israel carried out new strikes it insists are not subject to the truce.

image.altText: Smoke billows from a village

items.title: Whether they're faded, frayed or stained, every hat tells a story

children.content: For many, hats are more than a way to keep the sun off your face; they tell stories and hold memories from far and wide. Five men share their collections.

items.title: Aussies climb into podium contention as Vingegaard claims win

children.content: Jonas Vingegaard stamped his authority on the Giro d'Italia, but three Aussies are in the top 10 overall and all remain in consideration for the podium.

image.altText: Winner Jonas Vinegaard in yellow cycling suit holds up a giant wine glass in celebration.

items.title: 'Recipe for disaster': Road safety expert 'not surprised' by fatal Qld bus crash

children.content: A retired traffic engineer says Australia is "decades behind" in road safety standards and needs an overhaul after a second fatal bus crash on the same stretch of the Bruce Highway.

image.altText: A green bus on its side in grass after a crash. Emergency service workers are seen on the right hand side.

items.title: Voters head to polls for Stafford by-election

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focussed on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

items.title: Natural burials offer families peace at NSW conservation cemetery

children.content: After nearly 10 years getting public health and council approvals, a natural burial ground on the NSW south coast begins letting people have a simpler, more environmentally friendly farewell.

items.title: Scott Morrison joins historic bronze collection in avenue of honour

children.content: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.

image.altText: Scott Morrison standing next to a bronze bust of himself.

items.title: First memorial to Australia's deadliest Vietnam War battle unveiled

children.content: The longest and deadliest battle for Australian troops in the Vietnam War has finally been memorialised with a statue to the Battle of Coral–Balmoral erected in Gundagai.

image.altText: a bronze statue of two men in army uniforms, one carrying the other in a park
